topic.name: Federal Regulations and Policies
topic.description: This topic encompasses various federal regulations and policies regarding vehicle fleet management, land use, and energy infrastructure in the United States.

Document 1: Prohibits the number of limousines in the federal fleet for FY2015
Excepts any limousine determined to be essential for reasons of national security.
Document 2: Removes the temporary reservation for park, scenic, or other public purposes
Makes such rocks, pinnacles, reefs, and islands part of the California Coastal National Monument.
Document 3: Establishes the Commission on Comprehensive Strategies for the Placement of Natural Gas Infrastructure
conduct a comprehensive review of U.S. natural gas policy to identify problems.

topic.name: Cancer and Medicare Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on cancer screening, treatment, and Medicare-related healthcare services.

Document 1: Cancer Screening, Treatment, and Survivorship Act of 2007
requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services to make matching grants to states for cancer screening and referrals.
Document 2: Medicare Prescription Drug Savings and Choice Act of 2011
provides for establishment of Medicare operated prescription drug plan options.
Document 3: Medicare Transitional Care Act of 2012
covers transitional care services for qualified individuals provided by a transitional care clinician.

topic.name: Career Pathways and Education Funding
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative initiatives aimed at enhancing career and technical education opportunities and financial aid for students pursuing industry-recognized credentials and career pathways.

Document 1: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965 to expand the programs eligible to participate in federal student aid
makes eligible for Direct Loan program and Federal Perkins Loan program participation an educational program that provides at least 250 hours of instruction
Document 2: direct the Secretary of Education to include a space on the Free Application for Federal Student Aid (FAFSA) form to identify students who are or were in the foster care system
notify students who identify themselves as such of their potential eligibility for financial aid under title IV and other federal programs
Document 3: authorizes the Director of the National Academic and Career Innovation Center to award certain planning, implementation, transitional, and supplemental grants
create and enhance educational and career pathways for high school students to postsecondary schools

topic.name: Tariff Schedule Amendments
topic.description: The documents discuss temporary amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States related to specific products.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on digital zoom camera lenses.
Document 2: reduce temporarily the duty on frames and mountings for spectacles, goggles, or the like.
Document 3: modify the article description of Desmodur R-E
extend temporarily the suspension of duty on such article.

topic.name: Veterans Healthcare Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts aimed at improving healthcare access and quality for veterans in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Veterans Access, Choice, and Accountability Act of 2014
ensure that veterans enrolled in the health care system... are able to receive health care at VA medical facilities
Document 2: prohibits the Secretary of Veterans Affairs from altering the health care available to a veteran
amount of time that veteran has to wait for an appointment
Document 3: allows any veteran with a service-connected disability to receive hospital care or medical services
requires the cost of such care and services to be borne by the United States

topic.name: Alaska Oil and Gas Development
topic.description: Legislation and policies promoting oil and gas exploration and production in Alaska's National Petroleum Reserve and Coastal Plain.

Document 1: the National Petroleum Reserve (NPR) in Alaska remains explicitly designated to provide oil and natural gas resources
mandatory program of competitive leasing of oil and gas in the NPR
Document 2: exploration, leasing, development, production, and transportation of oil and gas in and from the Coastal Plain in Alaska
repeal the prohibition against production of oil and gas from Arctic National Wildlife Refuge
Document 3: no force or effect all federal prohibitions against the leasing of any area of the outer Continental Shelf
prepare a strategic five-year plan for the sale of oil and natural gas leases

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy and Enforcement
topic.description: This cluster addresses various aspects of U.S. immigration policy, including visa restrictions, employment verification, and penalties for unlawful presence.

Document 1: prohibits a consular officer from issuing an immigrant or nonimmigrant visa to any alien from countries with Ebola transmission
an alien from being admitted into the United States if the alien's journey included transit through such countries
Document 2: requires federal contractors and subcontractors to participate in the E-Verify Program
ensures that the Program is applied to all of the contractor's or subcontractor's employees
Document 3: amends the Immigration and Nationality Act to provide penalties for aliens lacking lawful immigration status
fines and/or imprisonment for unlawful presence in the United States

topic.name: EMP Weapons Accountability
topic.description: Legislation focused on the oversight and reporting of foreign electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness and Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the designation and protection of wilderness areas and national monuments in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes the Browns Canyon National Monument in Colorado to preserve the nationally significant biological, cultural, recreational, natural, wilderness, historic, scenic, riparian, geological, educational, and scientific values found within the National Monument.
Amends the Colorado Wilderness Act of 1993 to designate as the Browns Canyon Wilderness specified federal land in Chaffee County, Colorado.
Document 2: Designates specified wilderness areas in New Mexico and administered by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) as wilderness and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System (NWPS).
Establishes certain BLM-managed lands in the county as the Organ Mountains and Desert Peaks National Conservation Areas.

topic.name: Violence Against Women Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative efforts aimed at addressing and preventing violence against women, particularly in relation to specific vulnerable populations such as Native women and children.

Document 1: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to include sex trafficking as a target of the grants to Indian tribal governments to combat violent crime against Indian women.
Allows those grants to be used to address the needs of youth who are victims of, or exposed to, domestic violence, dating violence, sexual assault, sex trafficking, or stalking.
Document 2: This bill amends the federal criminal code to modify procedures for the payment of restitution and monetary assessments by defendants in certain child pornography cases.
The bill also establishes a Child Pornography Victim's Fund.
Document 3: Amends the Violence Against Women Act of 1994 to add or expand definitions of several terms for purposes of such Act.

Enhancing Judicial and Law Enforcement Tools to Combat Violence Against Women - Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to authorize appropriations through FY2017 for grants to combat violent crime against women (STOP grants).

topic.name: Health and Social Services Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving health and social services for specific populations, including individuals with autism, mental health needs, and elder abuse prevention.

Document 1: Amends the Public Health Service Act to revise provisions related to the Interagency Autism Coordinating Committee
Requires the Secretary of HHS to establish grant programs related to autism spectrum disorders
Document 2: Amends the Public Health Service Act to set forth criteria for the certification of federally-qualified community behavioral health centers
Authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to award matching grants to states or Indian tribes
Document 3: Amends the Older Americans Act of 1965 to direct the Administration on Aging of the Department of Health and Human Services
The Administration shall make grants to states with approved plans for elder abuse and neglect screening

topic.name: Drug Regulation and Control
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at regulating the approval, distribution, and combination of pharmaceutical drugs to address public health concerns.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to authorize the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to designate a combination of drugs as a significant drug combination

Requires the Secretary to review and take action on a drug in a significant drug combination designated under this Act within six months after receiving an application for approval of a new drug application
Document 2: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to include as a Schedule I controlled substance any material, compound, mixture, or preparation which contains specific designer drugs
Document 3: Ends the sale and distribution of pure hydrocodone bitartrate extended-release capsules
Prohibits the Commissioner of Food and Drugs (FDA) from approving any new drug application for the capsules unless they are formulated to prevent abuse

topic.name: Water Resource Management and Ecosystem Restoration
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative initiatives focused on the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities and ecosystem restoration projects across various regions in the United States.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with Pima County, Arizona, to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities
enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem
Document 2: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality
Comprehensive Everglades Restoration Plan
Document 3: authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with the city of San Juan Capistrano, California, to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility
expand reclaimed water distribution, storage, and treatment facilities

topic.name: Mortgage Regulation and Fraud Prevention
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at improving mortgage refinancing processes, ensuring compliance and protection for lenders and borrowers, and combating mortgage fraud.

Document 3: Requires the Director of the Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI) to assign the Chief of its Financial Crimes Section to be the Nationwide Mortgage Fraud Coordinator.
Directs the Coordinator to establish regional task forces to organize initiatives to address mortgage fraud.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the conservation and management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to the Secretary of Commerce.
Directs the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy.
Document 2: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function resulting from human activities.
Document 3: Amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds to appropriate projects.
Authorizes actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function from vessel impacts.

topic.name: Nutrition Assistance Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving nutrition assistance programs and education for children and families.

Document 1: Amends the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 to exclude the value of vehicles used for household transportation from the resource limitation for purposes of supplemental nutrition assistance eligibility.
Document 2: Amends the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to require local educational agencies to include nutrition education in their wellness policies.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture to implement a school-wide paperless free school meal program.

topic.name: Renewable Fuel Policy Amendments
topic.description: Legislative changes to the Clean Air Act and related acts that modify renewable fuel requirements and regulations in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Clean Air Act to allow any person that is unable to generate or purchase sufficient credits to meet renewable fuel requirements to carry forward a renewable fuel deficit
calendar year 2008, 2009, or 2010 for three years in lieu of the one calendar year currently allowed.
Document 2: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program
reducing the volume of renewable fuel that is required to be in gasoline sold or introduced into commerce in the United States to 7.5 billion gallons for each year.
Document 3: Amends the Clean Air Act to replace provisions prescribing the volume of renewable fuel that gasoline sold in the United States must contain
Requires such volume to be at least 10 billion gallons in 2010, 30 billion gallons in 2020, and 60 billion gallons in 2030.

topic.name: Privacy Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts aimed at protecting individuals' personal and identifiable information from unauthorized disclosure.

Document 1: Makes it unlawful for any officer or employee of the federal government to willfully make an unauthorized disclosure of personally identifiable covered information
Defines 'personally identifiable covered information' as return, return information, and taxpayer return information
Document 2: Prohibits the Department of Education from funding educational agencies or institutions that release student educational records without written parental consent
Defines 'authorized representative' for purposes of determining the individuals authorized to access student records
Document 3: Prohibits a nongovernmental individual or entity from knowingly collecting, obtaining, or disclosing geolocation information without express authorization
Defines 'geolocation information' as any information concerning the location of an electronic communications device

topic.name: Public Corruption and Tax Fraud Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at improving the prosecution and prevention of public corruption and tax-related crimes.

Document 1: establish a six-year limitation period for the prosecution of certain public corruption crimes
expand the penalties for theft or bribery involving programs receiving federal funds
Document 2: establish a Bureau of Criminal Investigation within the Department of the Treasury
provide a semi-annual report to Congress on cases referred to the bureau
Document 3: impose a fine and/or prison term on any person who knowingly or willfully misappropriates another person's tax identification number
require the Commissioner of Internal Revenue to submit an annual report on reported cases of tax fraud

topic.name: Small Business Banking Regulations
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing credit availability and regulatory relief for small banks and depository institutions.

Document 1: Amends the Commodity Exchange Act to exclude certain institutions from regulation as a swap dealer.
Exempts small banks from the clearing requirement.
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow a bank to have 500 shareholders and issue qualified preferred bank stock.
Document 3: Amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act to exempt institutions with total assets of $1 billion or less from certain rules.
Requires the CFPB to exempt certain loans held by smaller depository institutions from specific requirements.

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the conservation and management of wildlife species and their habitats in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Wetlands Loan Act to increase and extend funding for conservation of migratory waterfowl
Expresses the sense of Congress that the funds generated pursuant to this Act should be used for preserving and increasing waterfowl populations
Document 2: adds the Burmese Python of the species Python molurus bivittatus and the African Rock Python of the species Python sebae to the list of injurious species
Document 3: Amends the Endangered Species Act of 1973 to require the consent of the governor of a state for a determination of endangered or threatened species status
Establishes a process for exclusive state regulation of endangered or threatened species determined by a state governor

topic.name: Federal Land Designation and Conveyance
topic.description: This topic encompasses the designation of federal lands for various purposes, including wilderness preservation and public use conveyance.

Document 1: Designates specified federal land within Olympic National Park, Washington, as wilderness or potential wilderness.
Incorporates such land in the Olympic Wilderness.
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of General Services (GSA) to offer to convey to Teton County, Wyoming, the parcel of land located at 145 East Simpson Street.
Requires the deed for conveyance to include a covenant providing that the parcel and building will be for public use.
Document 3: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 152 North 5th Street in Laramie, Wyoming, as the 'Gale W. McGee Post Office.'

topic.name: High-Speed Rail Development
topic.description: Legislation and initiatives aimed at expanding and improving high-speed rail infrastructure and services in the United States.

Document 1: tax credit for 25% of the cost of new qualified freight rail infrastructure property
expensing election after 2012
Document 2: authorize states to return unobligated stimulus funds for high speed rail
Secretary to deposit such funds into the general fund
Document 3: establish high-speed rail systems in the United States
grants to finance high-speed capital projects
requires state rail plan for grant approval

topic.name: STEM Education and Policy
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at enhancing science, technology, engineering, and mathematics education and research in the United States.

Document 1: Establishes, revises, and extends specified science, technology, engineering, and mathematics (STEM) programs
Requires the OSTP to develop consistent federal policies for recipients of federal research awards who have caregiving responsibilities
Document 2: Requires the Director of the Office of Science and Technology Policy (OSTP) to establish a committee under the National Science and Technology Council
identify and coordinate international science and technology cooperation that can strengthen U.S. science and technology enterprise
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of Education to establish and maintain a database of information on public and private programs of financial assistance for the study of postsecondary and graduate science, technology, engineering, and mathematics

topic.name: Tax-Advantaged Accounts Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to tax benefits and regulations for various types of savings accounts, including IRAs and accounts for individuals with disabilities.

Document 1: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow tax free rollovers of military death gratuities to Roth individual retirement accounts
health savings and Archer medical savings accounts
Document 2: Suspends for calendar 2008 and 2009 the beginning date for required distributions from certain individual retirement (IRA) plans.
Document 3: Establish tax-exempt ABLE accounts to assist an individual with a disability
Defines 'qualified disability expenses' to include expenses for education, including higher education expenses.

topic.name: Small Business Legislative Initiatives
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative acts aimed at improving and supporting small businesses in various sectors, particularly through the Small Business Administration.

Document 1: amends the Small Business Act
require the Small Business Administration (SBA) to provide a list of all changes made in the prior year to regulations
Document 2: Directs the Administrator of the Small Business Administration (SBA) to establish a program to guarantee loans of small businesses
carry out the program in a manner similar to the SBA 7(a) general business loan program
Document 3: amends the Small Business Act to expand the definition of 'small business concern owned and controlled by service-disabled veterans'
make the eligibility requirements for participation in veteran-owned small business contracting programs consistent for both SBA programs and VA programs

topic.name: Native American Education and Land Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the education and land rights of Native American tribes, focusing on language preservation and community engagement through service programs.

Document 3: Authorize the Secretary of Education to award grants to tribes, tribal colleges or universities, tribal education agencies, schools, and private or tribal nonprofit organizations to develop and maintain, or improve and expand, programs that support the use by schools, from prekindergarten through postsecondary education, of Native American languages as their primary language of instruction.
Requires grantees to support Native American language education and development.

topic.name: Social Security and Disability Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at improving access to Social Security benefits and support for disabled individuals and seniors.

Document 1: Ending the Medicare Disability Waiting Period Act of 2009
eliminate the waiting period for individuals with life-threatening conditions
Document 2: CPI for Seniors Act of 2011
Consumer Price Index for Seniors (CPI-S)
Document 3: School Attendance Improves Lives Act of 2015
condition SSI benefits for disabled children aged 16 or 17 on school attendance

topic.name: Regulatory Reform in Education and Employment
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative efforts aimed at reforming federal regulations related to education and employment, focusing on transparency, cost analysis, and the impacts on job creation.

Document 1: Prohibits the Secretary of Education from issuing a final rule...
requires a complete data analysis on the impact of such proposed rule
Document 2: Establishes the Office of Regulatory Analysis in the executive branch...
requires agencies to provide the information that the Director requests
Document 3: Prohibits a federal agency from taking any significant regulatory action...
Permits an agency to take a significant regulatory action notwithstanding the freeze

topic.name: Iran Proliferation Sanctions
topic.description: The documents discuss various U.S. legislative measures aimed at imposing sanctions and restrictions on Iran's nuclear and military proliferation activities.

Document 1: States that specified U.S. sanctions with respect to Iran... shall remain in effect until the President certifies...
Establishes restrictions on nuclear cooperation with a country that is assisting the nuclear program of... Iran.
Document 2: Subjects Iran to specified economic sanctions unless the President certifies to Congress that Iran has verifiably dismantled all nuclear enrichment-related and reprocessing-related programs.
Amends the Iran Sanctions Act of 1996 to expand the definitions of 'petroleum resources' and 'person.'
Document 3: States that the property interest of Iran in: a blocked asset shall include an interest in property of any nature whatsoever...
States that property of Iran shall be deemed to be property in the United States of Iran if that property is...

topic.name: Housing and Child Welfare Policy
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative measures aimed at improving housing stability and welfare support for vulnerable children and families.

Document 1: a child may not be placed in foster care under state responsibility solely because the family with which the child is living is homeless
the state will work with the family and state housing authorities to secure permanent housing for any family that includes a minor child and is homeless
Document 2: to allow a student who is a homeless child or youth or a student who is a homeless veteran to occupy low-income housing units
Document 3: require that each housing assistance payments contract...provide that such owner pay, on a timely basis, all covered taxes validly assessed against the property
the PHA shall abate all of the rental assistance amounts for the property, transferring them monthly to the taxing authority, until the delinquency is eliminated

topic.name: Campaign Finance and Lobbying Disclosure
topic.description: Legislation focused on enhancing transparency and compliance in campaign finance and lobbying activities.

Document 1: Requires the Attorney General to establish the Lobbying Disclosure Act Enforcement Task Force.
Amends the Lobbying Disclosure Act of 1995 to require notifications of noncompliance of lobbyist disclosure requirements to the Attorney General.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 and the Internal Revenue Code to set forth requirements for the disclosure of independent federal election-related contributions and expenditures.
Defines 'independent federal election-related activity expenditure' to mean any expenditure that influences or attempts to influence the nomination or election of any individual to federal office.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Election Campaign Act of 1971 to revise the requirement of a 48-hour notification of a campaign contribution of $1,000 or more to the Federal Election Commission.
Requires Senate candidates to file designations, statements, and reports directly with the FEC.

topic.name: Consumer Protection Legislation
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative measures aimed at safeguarding consumer rights in financial and pharmaceutical transactions.

Document 1: permits the dispensing of drugs only pursuant to a 'valid prescription'
requires the Secretary of Health and Human Services (HHS) to establish a Registry of Legitimate Online Pharmacy Websites
Document 2: amends the Truth in Lending Act to cover any qualified small business as a consumer
prohibits discrimination against any business concern having 50 or fewer employees
Document 3: prohibits a creditor from making an extension of credit to a consumer with respect to which the annual percentage credit rate (APR) exceeds 36%
empowers states' Attorneys General to enforce it

topic.name: Telecommunications Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on legislative measures related to telecommunications, including consumer education and regulation of broadband and digital television services.

Document 1: impose criminal penalties on anyone who knowingly purchases and/or handles one or more wireless prepaid access devices
engages in the unauthorized resale or redistribution outside of the United States
Document 2: require retail distributors of television receiving equipment to place adjacent to each unit displayed for sale a consumer alert
directs the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to create a DTV Transition Federal Advisory Committee
Document 3: direct the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to establish a process under which an incumbent service provider providing broadband service to a remote rural area may submit to the Secretary information
assess whether a submitted application meets appropriate eligibility requirements

topic.name: Military Benefits Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures aimed at enhancing the benefits and entitlements of military service members and their families.

Document 1: monetary allowance for a member of the Armed Forces for travel by privately owned vehicles
transport at government expense of one motor vehicle owned or leased by a member
Document 2: clear and comprehensive statement of the medical care and treatment to which the member is entitled
provided each time the member is mobilized or demobilized
Document 3: annuity payments to a surviving child of an eligible participant
exempting such payments from the alternative minimum tax

topic.name: Public Health and Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on ensuring the safety and health standards of products and systems that impact public health.

Document 1: Drinking Water Disaster Act of 2016
contamination of drinking water from a public water system.
Document 2: Robin Danielson Feminine Hygiene Product Safety Act of 2015
research on the extent to which components...pose any risks to the health of women.
Document 3: Children's Products Safety Act of 2007
require every manufacturer...to certify...that the product conforms to such standard.

topic.name: Labor Relations and Employee Rights
topic.description: This cluster addresses legislative measures aimed at enhancing labor relations, employee rights, and collective bargaining processes.

Document 1: Amends the National Labor Relations Act (NLRA) to revise requirements for National Labor Relation Board (NLRB) determination of an appropriate bargaining unit.
Requires the NLRB to determine a unit as appropriate for collective bargaining if it consists of employees that share a sufficient community of interest.
Document 2: Grants an employee the right to request that his or her employer change the terms and conditions of employment relating to the number of hours or times the employee is required to work.
Requires the employer, if the request is made, to engage in a timely, good faith interactive process with the employee.

topic.name: Guantanamo Bay Detention Policy
topic.description: Legislation regarding the detention and transfer of individuals at Guantanamo Bay, focusing on preventing terrorism and ensuring transparency in detainee management.

Document 1: prohibits U.S. agency funds from being used to transfer an individual detained at Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay

expresses the sense of Congress that any individual who was detained at Guantanamo and is suspected of or confirmed by U.S. intelligence as having re-engaged in terrorist activity after being released should be arrested and returned to detention at Guantanamo.
Document 2: reaffirms that the U.S. Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba is and shall be a location for the detention of individuals in the custody or control of the Department of Defense

prohibits any federal funds from being used to construct or modify any facility in the United States or its territories or possessions to house any individual in the custody or control of DOD or under detention at Guantanamo.
Document 3: expresses the sense of Congress that the people of the United States deserve to know who is being released from the detention facilities at U.S. Naval Station, Guantanamo Bay, Cuba
the Department of State shall submit to Congress an unclassified notice that includes the name, country of origin, and country of destination of the individual.

topic.name: Education Policy and Improvement
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at enhancing educational environments and addressing issues such as bullying, early childhood education, and middle school improvement.

Document 1: Safe Schools Improvement Act of 2009
require states to collect and report information on the incidence of bullying and harassment
Document 2: Universal Prekindergarten and Early Childhood Education Act of 2011
allow local educational agencies to use funds for educational activities for children four years of age or younger
Document 3: Success in the Middle Act of 2007
directs the Secretary of Education to make matching grants to states for middle school improvement plans

topic.name: Voting Rights Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on ensuring fair access to voting and the integrity of the electoral process in the United States.

Document 1: prohibit a state from imposing additional conditions or requirements on the eligibility of an individual to cast a vote in federal elections by mail
deadline for requesting the ballot and returning it
Document 2: voter verification and mandatory auditing by state officials
prohibition of deceptive practices and certain campaign activities
Document 3: conduct a plebiscite giving voters the option to vote to continue Puerto Rico's present political status or to have a different political status
certify plebiscite results to the President and Congress

topic.name: Firearms Legislation and Rights
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative measures related to firearms ownership, usage rights, and regulatory reforms in the United States.

Document 1: Revises the civil penalties for violations of firearms law
Prohibits ATF from disclosing information on firearms purchasers
Document 2: A member of the Armed Forces assigned to duty at an Armed Forces recruitment office has the right to carry a personal or service-issue firearm
Document 3: Prohibits the Secretary of the Army from promulgating or enforcing any regulation that prohibits an individual from possessing a firearm

topic.name: Aviation Regulation and Training
topic.description: This cluster focuses on the regulatory framework and training requirements associated with aviation operations and services in the United States.

Document 1: Repeals the Essential Air Service Local Participation Program.
Establishes within the Office of the Secretary the Office of Rural Aviation.
Document 2: Revises requirements with respect to the training of aliens and other individuals designated by the Secretary of Homeland Security in the operation of certain aircraft.
Directs the Secretary to require Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) certification of flight schools.
Document 3: Authorizes the Secretary of the Air Force, for purposes of modernizing the C-130 aircraft fleet.
Requires the Secretary to ensure that all C-130 aircraft are capable of meeting applicable regulations of the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) by January 1, 2020.

topic.name: Food Safety Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at ensuring the safety and traceability of meat and food products in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act to include in the definition of 'adulterated' any carcass or part thereof...
contaminated with enterohemorrhagic (EHEC) Shiga toxin-producing serotypes of Escherichia coli (E. coli).
Document 2: Amends the Federal Meat Inspection Act to direct the Secretary of Agriculture to establish a traceability system...
for all stages of manufacturing, processing, packaging, and distribution of food.
Document 3: Amends the Federal Food, Drug, and Cosmetic Act to impose a fine and/or prison term of up to 10 years for knowingly violating such Act...
with respect to food that is misbranded or adulterated.

topic.name: Support for Minority Institutions
topic.description: Legislative measures aimed at enhancing educational and health opportunities for minority-serving institutions and communities.

Document 1: Establishes within the National Science Foundation (NSF) an Office of Minority Serving Institution Digital and Wireless Technology
Requires the NSF Director to establish an advisory council on the best approaches for involving eligible institutions
Document 2: Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to provide full reimbursement for medical assistance given to a Native Hawaiian
through a federally-qualified health center or a Native Hawaiian health care system.

topic.name: Employment Rights Legislation
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at protecting the employment rights of servicemembers and enhancing transparency in accounting oversight.

Document 1: amends the Uniformed Services Employment Rights Act of 1994
make any agreement to arbitrate a claim under such provisions unenforceable
Document 2: amends the Uniformed Services Employment Rights Act of 1994
make any agreement to arbitrate a claim under such provisions unenforceable
Document 3: Amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002
make Public Company Accounting Oversight Board disciplinary proceedings public

topic.name: USPS Commemorative and Financial Services
topic.description: Legislation related to the commemorative actions and expanded financial services of the United States Postal Service.

Document 1: Commemorative postage stamp in honor of Ebenezer D. Bassett.
Document 2: Allows the United States Postal Service (USPS) to accept monetary donations for the funding of plaques.
Prohibits construing this Act to affect the authority of the USPS regarding requirements concerning the design, placement, and limitation on costs relating to commemorative plaques.

topic.name: Gang Prevention and Tribal Justice
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislative measures aimed at preventing gang activity and enhancing tribal justice systems through community-based programs and courts.

Document 1: Authorizes any local or tribal government to submit an application to the Attorney General for designation as a High Intensity Gang Activity Area.
Directs the Attorney General to establish an Interagency Gang Prevention Task Force in each Area.
Document 2: This bill permits the Department of Justice (DOJ) to award grants to tribal governments acting in partnership with state or local governments for tribal healing to wellness courts.
Courts receiving grants must monitor individuals under their jurisdiction and must periodically test each individual for drug use.
Document 3: Amends the Omnibus Crime Control and Safe Streets Act of 1968 to allow funds provided under the Matching Grant Program for School Security to be used for law enforcement and school information sharing activities.
Enabling law enforcement to consult with schools to develop emergency plans.

topic.name: Abortion Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at regulating or restricting abortion practices in the United States.

Document 1: prohibit the Department of Health and Human Service (HHS) from providing federal family planning assistance to an entity unless the entity certifies that, during the period of assistance, the entity will not perform, and will not provide funds to any other entity that performs, an abortion.
Requires HHS to provide Congress annually information on grantees who performed abortions under the exceptions.
Document 2: Prohibits a federal official from expending federal funds for any foreign or domestic population control or population planning program or family planning activity (including any abortion procedure).

to require a program under which the Secretary of Veterans Affairs provides chiropractic care and services to veterans.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an income-based tax credit for 50% of qualified infertility treatment expenses
Defines 'qualified infertility treatment expenses' as amounts paid for the treatment of infertility via in vitro fertilization.

topic.name: Education and Support Services
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislation aimed at improving educational environments and support services for specific populations, particularly in reducing disciplinary actions in schools and enhancing transportation options for veterans.

Document 1: Amends the school improvement program under part A of title I of the Elementary and Secondary Education Act of 1965 (ESEA) to require state school improvement plans to contain assurances that the state will work with other education stakeholders to reduce suspensions and expulsions.
Requires the school improvement plans of local educational agencies (LEAs) to include a description of the actions the LEA will take to reduce suspensions and expulsions.

topic.name: Government Financial Initiatives
topic.description: This topic encompasses various legislative acts focused on financial incentives and funding mechanisms to support economic development and public access in different sectors.

Document 1: United States Commission on an Open Society with Security Act - Establishes the United States Commission on an Open Society with Security to study how the government may provide for both security in, and public access to, federal buildings and other federal property and sites.
public access to federal buildings and other federal property
Document 2: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow individual taxpayers a one-time tax credit for up to $10,000 of the cost of a principal residence purchased in the United States
a one-time tax credit for up to $10,000 of the cost of a principal residence
Document 3: Southern Africa Enterprise Development Fund Act of 2008 - Authorizes: (1) the Southern Africa Enterprise Development Fund (SAEDF) to conduct public offerings or private placements for the purpose of soliciting and accepting venture capital
financial returns on SAEDF investments that include a component of private venture capital to be distributed to the investors of such capital.

topic.name: Tribal Governance and Law
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the governance, legal frameworks, and public safety initiatives affecting Native American tribes.

Document 1: amends the Indian Law Enforcement Act
establish various public safety programs and services with respect to Indian communities
Document 2: Authorizes the Mescalero Apache Tribe to lease or transfer water rights
complies with the laws of New Mexico
Document 3: requires the head of each federal agency to describe the implementation of continuous process improvement
improve process speed, reduce waste, and incorporate requirements with data driven project analysis
